title: Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike
content:
{{Top|rwm|reb|italics=1}} {{Youmay|the [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Rogue Squadron'']] sequel|the ''[[Star Wars Rebels]]'' [[Star Wars Rebels: Rebel Strike|browser game]]|linebreak=1}} {{VideoGame |image=[[File:SWroguesquad3.jpg]] |title=''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III:
Rebel Strike'' |developer=[[Factor 5]] |publisher=[[LucasArts]] |release date=[[October 14]], [[2003]]{{SWArchive|url=gaming/videogames/news/news20031003.html|text=Rebels Strike Back with Bonus Disc}} |closed= |upc= |engine= |genre=*Action *Flight-simulator |modes=*Single-player *Co-operative campaign *Split-screen multiplayer |ratings=[[Wikipedia:Entertainment Software Rating Board|ESRB]]: Teen (T), [[Wikipedia:Pan European Game Information|PEGI]]: 12+
[[Wikipedia:Unterhaltungssoftware Selbstkontrolle|USK]]: 12 |platforms=[[Wikipedia:Nintendo GameCube|GameCube]] |series=[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (series)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'' video game series]] |basegame= |expansions= |director=[[Julian Eggebrecht]] |producer=[[Brett Tosti]] |designer= |programmer= |artist= |writer= |composer=[[Chris Hülsbeck]] |timeline=[[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]]–[[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]]''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' |prev=''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader]]'' |next= }} '''''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike''''' is the third entry in the [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (series)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] series, released in [[2003]] exclusively for the [[Wikipedia:Nintendo GameCube|Nintendo GameCube]], developed by [[Factor 5]] and published by [[LucasArts]]. It is currently a Player's Choice title. It added to its predecessors the ability for the player to depart their [[Starship/Legends|starship]] and join a land battle as well as enter land vehicles such as an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] and [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|AT-ST]] during certain missions. The game is divided into two campaigns, [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles's]] and [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker's]], plus a [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] campaign where the player takes control of Luke, [[Arvel Crynyd/Legends|Arvel Crynyd]], and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]]. It also has the campaign of the previous game in multiplayer co-op mode and various VS modes. ==Opening crawl== {{OpeningCrawl |name=Rogue Squadron III
REBEL STRIKE |text=It is a dark time for the Rebellion. Though the Death Star has been destroyed, the Empire remains intent on crushing the Alliance. A massive Imperial fleet has invaded the Rebel base on Yavin 4 in an attempt to capture the Rebel leaders, enacting swift retribution for the Alliance's earlier victory. In the face of this assault, a small squad of freedom fighters led by the heroic LUKE SKYWALKER and WEDGE ANTILLES attempt to escape the planet for a new secret base....}} ==Plot summary== During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the ace pilots of [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] fought in numerous battles, from the early [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] to the pivotal [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]]. Led by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Wedge Antilles/Legends|Wedge Antilles]], the squadron proved instrumental in the war. {{Plot}} Luke Skywalker led Rogue Squadron in numerous battles, from [[Mission to Dantooine|rescuing]] [[Tycho Celchu/Legends|defectors]] to [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|fighting]] on the icy plains of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. After Skywalker left to become a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]], Antilles took over the command of the squadron and led them on several secret [[Mission to Destrillion|missions]]. ==Gameplay== [[File:SWRSIIIRSWP.jpg|thumb|200px|Promotional image from ''Rebel Strike'']] ''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike'' is a space-combat simulator, like the other entries in the [[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (series)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'' series]]. For the first time in the series, it is possible to play missions on foot or in a ground vehicle. ===Levels=== *[[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] Training *[[Attack on Yavin 4|Revenge of the Empire]] ====Left campaign (Luke)==== *[[Mission to Dantooine|Defiance on Dantooine]] *[[Defense of Ralltiir|Defenders of Ralltiir]] *[[Defense of Ralltiir|Extraction]] from [[Ralltiir/Legends|Ralltiir]] *[[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battlefield Hoth]] *Trials of a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] *[[Sarlacc/Legends|Sarlacc]] Pit ([[Battle of the Great Pit of Carkoon]]) ====Right campaign (Wedge)==== *[[Raid at Bakura]] *[[Battle of Geonosis (Galactic Civil War)|Relics of Geonosis]] *[[Mission to Destrillion|Deception at Destrillion]] *[[Battle of Dubrillion (Galactic Civil War)|Guns of Dubrillion]] *[[Fondor shipyard assault|Fondor Shipyard Assault]] ====Final levels==== *Speeder Bike Pursuit *[[Battle of Endor/Legends|Triumph of the Rebellion]] ===Bonus missions=== *[[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]] [[Rescue of Princess Leia/Legends|Rescue]] *Escape from [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] *[[Occupation of Bespin|Flight]] from [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]] *[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] Endurance *Attack on the [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']] ===Multiplayer=== There are five different multiplayer game types: Dogfight, Rampage, Tag and Defend, Special, and co-op. *Dogfight is a simple one-on-one battle in the spacecraft of your choice. *Rampage involves players getting points via destroying things. *Tag and Defend is the most complicated. Players must control certain buildings. You generate 1 point per 15 seconds per building you control. Buildings you control automatically begin building defenses. You can capture enemy buildings by destroying them. Whoever reaches the point limit first wins. *Special is composed of races through well-known ''Star Wars'' locales and and a version of Rampage but with ground vehicles. *Co-op is a campaign style of play. Most of the co-op levels are single-player levels from ''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron II: Rogue Leader]]'', with minor changes. ==Continuity== The three [[Arcade game/Legends|arcade games]] based on the original trilogy are included as bonuses. [[Star Wars: The Arcade Game|''Star Wars'']] is unlocked after finishing "[[Rescue of Princess Leia/Legends|Death Star Rescue]]," [[The Empire Strikes Back (arcade game)|''The Empire Strikes Back'']] after "[[Battle of Endor/Legends|Triumph of the Rebellion]]". [[Return of the Jedi (arcade game)|''Return of the Jedi'']] is only available through a cheat code (specifically, "!?ATH!RD GAME?YES"). After the player beats the game, the unlockable special feature ''[[The Making of Star Wars: Rogue Squadron III: Rebel Strike]]'' can be opened. ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|from=1|imagecat=Images from
